level_signals: 7+ years of progressive experience in Product Management
Demonstrated success launching and scaling complex products in enterprise environment
Expected to guide architecture and investment decisions and interact with CTO/Engineering leads

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years of progressive experience in Product Management, ideally within an enterprise or B2B setting.
Demonstrated success in defining, launching, and scaling complex products from ideation to general availability in an enterprise environment.
Proven ability to manage diverse stakeholders (Engineering, Design, Sales, Marketing, Executive Leadership) and drive cross-functional alignment on product vision and prioritization.
Exceptional ability to map, analyze, and refine Customer User Journeys (CUJs) to identify high-value pain points and opportunities for AI solution deployment.

Highly data-driven approach to prioritization and decision-making, including the ability to define key performance indicators (KPIs), create product impact dashboards, and synthesize data into actionable product requirements.
A track record of leveraging qualitative (customer interviews) and quantitative (product usage data) insights to validate assumptions and drive product strategy.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to articulate complex technical concepts and product strategies clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Hands-on experience developing and launching AI/ML-powered products or features for internal or external customers.
Robust functional and technical understanding of Large Language Models (LLMs) and common AI-based workflows (e.g., retrieval-augmented generation, fine-tuning, prompt engineering).
Prior experience working directly with LLMs or launching conversational AI agents into production.
Technical background (e.g., Computer Science degree or equivalent practical experience) that allows for deep engagement with engineering teams on technical trade-offs and architectural decisions.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Track record of solving high-impact, cross-system challenges and aligning senior stakeholders across Product, Engineering, and Core infrastructure organizations.

scope.surface: Stripe Infrastructure and Data Experiences (IDX) — including Core Infra, Developer Infra, Data Platform, Service Platform, Seller Systems, and internal AI agents
primary_metrics_inferred: International market expansion via local infrastructure
cross_functional_partners: Engineering (Core Infra, Developer Infra, Data Platform, Service Platform)
core_responsibilities: Partner with engineering teams to ship functionality that delights users and makes a measurable impact on the business
Establish system and design principles for Seller Systems — build vs. buy, modernization vs. extension, platform vs. point solution
Interact with external users and propose critical infrastructure abstractions
Validate product ideas and prototypes with early adopters
Define key success metrics for developer products
Work on infrastructure, API, data models/pipelines roadmaps
Work on internal AI agents for functions such as Recruiting, Legal etc.

green_flags: Clear product surface with named engineering partner teams (Core Infra, Developer Infra, Data Platform, Service Platform)
Explicit encouragement to apply if minimum requirements are met
High-impact scope: unlocking international markets and enterprise-grade experiences
AI/ML and LLM work is substantive and production-oriented, not superficial
Role partners directly with Stripe's CTO and engineering leads — high visibility
red_flags: Location and work arrangement not stated anywhere in the JD — unclear if remote, hybrid, or onsite
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not mentioned
7+ year minimum is a relatively high bar that may filter out strong mid-level candidates
JD blends infrastructure PM and AI PM responsibilities broadly, making the actual focus somewhat ambiguous

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 7+ years requirement and scope of responsibilities)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description and enterprise/B2B context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed as metrics in JD)
ambiguities: The JD does not specify a work location or remote/hybrid/onsite policy anywhere — location.type is marked as unclear

The 'ideally within an enterprise or B2B setting' qualifier on the 7+ years requirement suggests it is a preference rather than hard requirement, but it appears under Minimum Requirements; treated as hard with qualifier noted
AI & Technical Expertise section is listed under 'Minimum requirements' in the JD structure but is a separate subsection — all items treated as hard requirements per JD placement
